surface.name: Sauce Labs free-trial Selenium Java DesktopTests
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Selected the fully reconstructed Selenium Java DesktopTests candidate because it is the complete atomic first-test path already recorded in this file.

alternatives_considered.name: Selenium automated web test
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The guide is a complete seven-part test pattern, but Sauce Labs recommends WebdriverIO for JavaScript and also documents Java, C#, Python, and Ruby variants.

alternatives_considered.name: Live web or mobile test
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Live testing is a separate browser-driven surface with different setup and verification from automated tests.

alternatives_considered.name: Framework quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The index requires the developer to select a framework, target platform, and language.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Sauce Labs trial account and, if Selenium Java is selected, run the linked example test through its first visible Sauce Labs result.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
The developer has Git, GitHub access, JDK 11 or later, Maven, and an IDE for the documented Selenium Java candidate.
The application type, live versus automated mode, framework, language, and browser or device target have not been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Completed Selenium job visible in Automated Test Results
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: DesktopTests runs on Sauce Labs and its completed job and assets appear in Automated Test Results.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The completed job appears in Automated Test Results with its result and assets.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The completed job appears in Automated Test Results with its result and assets.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the documented remote-side completion signal of the committed Selenium Java candidate under workshop policy.

prerequisites.requirement: Select live or automated testing, web or mobile target, framework, and implementation language. The atomic candidate recorded below selects Selenium with Java.
A Sauce Labs account is required. The current signup page offers a no-credit-card 28-day trial with 60 automated and live testing minutes and two virtual machines in parallel.
The documented Selenium Java example requires Git, GitHub access, JDK 11 or later, Maven, and an IDE.
Automated tests require the account username and access key, preferably provided as SAUCE_USERNAME and SAUCE_ACCESS_KEY environment variables.
The data center selected during signup determines the Remote WebDriver endpoint and where test artifacts are stored.

primary_path.action: Open the free-trial signup page and choose Google, GitHub, or email signup.
details: The page says no credit card is required.
The trial is 28 days and includes 60 automated and live testing minutes plus two virtual machines in parallel.
primary_path.input: An existing Google or GitHub identity, or an email address.
primary_path.output: The selected identity-provider flow or email registration form.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An organization-managed identity provider can add its own authentication or approval requirements.

primary_path.action: For email signup, enter a business email and username, create a compliant password, choose United States or Europe as the test data-center location, accept the notices, and submit Sign up.
details: The password requires at least eight characters, one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, one digit, and one special character.
The page links the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy, and the data-center choice determines where tests run.
primary_path.input: Business email, username, compliant password, selected region, and acceptance of the linked terms and privacy notice.
primary_path.output: A Sauce Labs free-trial account in the selected data center.
primary_path.success_signal: The account is created and the developer can enter Sauce Labs.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid password composition, an unavailable username, an existing account, CAPTCHA, or identity verification can block submission.

primary_path.action: Choose the Selenium automated web-testing route and its linked Java example repository.
details: The documentation router has no universal first test.
This candidate follows the Selenium Quickstart link to Sauce Labs' maintained demo-java repository.
primary_path.input: A decision to use Selenium with Java rather than another documented test route.
primary_path.success_signal: The repository setup and run instructions are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting a different framework or product surface changes the remainder of the path.

primary_path.action: Clone the example repository with git clone https://github.com/saucelabs-training/demo-java.git and import it into the IDE.
details: The repository requires Git, JDK 11 or later, Maven, and an IDE.
Enable the Java module of interest so Maven recognizes it.
primary_path.input: Local Git, JDK, Maven, IDE, and GitHub access.
primary_path.output: A local demo-java checkout recognized by the IDE.
primary_path.success_signal: The repository modules are visible and Maven can address them.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing Git, JDK, Maven, IDE module configuration, or network access prevents setup.

primary_path.action: Open User Settings and copy the Sauce Labs username and access key.
details: The account data center shown in the UI selects the matching endpoint.
The credentials should not be hard-coded into the example.
primary_path.output: Username, access key, and selected data center.
primary_path.success_signal: Both credential values are available for local configuration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The wrong account or region can produce invalid credentials or an inaccessible endpoint.

primary_path.action: Set SAUCE_USERNAME and SAUCE_ACCESS_KEY in the local shell, then echo both variables to verify they are present.
details: Use export on macOS or Linux and set or System Properties on Windows.
If macOS shell variables are not visible to the process, the repository documents launchctl setenv as a recovery.
primary_path.input: Copied Sauce Labs username and access key.
primary_path.output: Credential environment variables available to the test process.
primary_path.success_signal: The verification commands print non-empty values.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Empty output, a new terminal without persisted variables, or shell quoting errors prevent authentication.

primary_path.action: Enter the best-practice module, run mvn dependency:resolve, then run mvn test-compile.
details: The first command resolves package dependencies.
The second compiles the example test code before execution.
primary_path.input: Local demo-java checkout with JDK and Maven available.
primary_path.output: Resolved dependencies and compiled test code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dependency download failures, Java version mismatch, proxy restrictions, or compile errors stop the path before a remote session.

primary_path.action: Run mvn test -Dtest=DesktopTests to start the documented remote Selenium example.
details: The test uses the configured Sauce credentials and a Remote WebDriver session.
A data-center endpoint outside the account entitlement produces an access or concurrency error.
primary_path.input: Compiled example, credential environment variables, account region, and available trial minutes and concurrency.
primary_path.output: A remote Selenium job and local Maven test result.
primary_path.success_signal: Maven reports the example test result and the Remote WebDriver session ends.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication, wrong endpoint, unavailable concurrency, unsupported capability, dependency, or assertion failures can prevent completion.

primary_path.action: Open Automated > Test Results and inspect the completed Selenium job and its video or screenshot assets.
details: The result UI is the documented remote-side completion signal.
The free trial includes screenshots and videos of tests.
primary_path.output: Visible completed test record and assets in Sauce Labs.
primary_path.success_signal: The completed job appears in Automated Test Results with its result and assets.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A failed launch creates no completed job; a test may also need time to finish and publish assets.

branches.condition: Automated Selenium web testing is selected.
branches.path: Continue through steps 4 through 9 for the fully decomposed Java example. The broader Selenium guide also documents Java, C#, Python, and Ruby variants, but their setup and commands are not silently substituted into this candidate.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The first observable result is a completed automated Selenium job in the Sauce Labs UI.

branches.condition: A JavaScript automated web test is selected.
branches.path: Follow the framework-specific WebdriverIO, Cypress, Playwright, TestCafe, or other documented quickstart rather than silently translating the Selenium-language guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Installation, configuration, execution command, and result reporting become framework-specific.

branches.condition: Live, mobile, visual, performance, or error-reporting testing is selected.
branches.path: Enter the matching product guide and follow its route-specific application access, device or browser selection, session launch or instrumentation, and verification steps.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The terminal signal and setup gates differ from an automated web test.

friction_gates.description: Email signup requires business email, username, a password meeting five composition rules, a United States or Europe data-center choice, and acceptance of the linked notices.
Test mode, application type, framework, language, target, and data center must be selected.
Automated routes require a Sauce Labs username and access key, with environment variables recommended.
Framework-specific local dependencies and a data-center-specific endpoint must match the chosen route.
Private or local applications can require a Sauce Trusted Connection route before the cloud browser or device can reach them.
A remote browser or device session must become available and the selected test must finish before a result can be inspected.
The Selenium candidate requires both the test assertion result and the completed job in Automated Test Results.

sources.title: Sauce Labs Documentation
sections_used: Quickstart with your favorite test suite

sources.title: Selenium on Sauce Labs
sections_used: Step 2: Navigate to a Web Page

Sign Up for a Free Trial
evidence_supported: Google, GitHub, and email signup routes
United States or Europe data-center selection

sources.title: Sauce Labs Java Demonstration Scripts
evidence_supported: Vendor-owned saucelabs-training repository linked by the official Selenium Quickstart
Git, JDK 11+, Maven, and IDE prerequisites
Dependency, compile, and DesktopTests execution commands

uncertainties.question: Which Sauce Labs product surface, framework, language, and target should define first success?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes substantive steps after account creation, including dependencies, capabilities, application access, execution, wait, and result verification. Steps 3 through 9 record only the documented Selenium Java candidate.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation is a router and explicitly asks developers to choose a favorite suite; it does not nominate one universal first test.

excluded_after_success: Additional browsers, devices, and operating systems
Sauce Connect setup unless the selected application is private
Visual, performance, accessibility, and error-reporting expansion
Test analytics, annotations, and organization administration
